items.title: Man accused of ramming, shooting at car in Canberra's north

children.content: The 20-year-old's defence lawyer said his client was needed at a barber shop, but Magistrate James Stewart said his circumstances did not meet the threshold for bail.

items.title: Canberra man jailed over sexual abuse of 'extremely vulnerable' child

children.content: A man who sexually assaulted a 12-year-old girl after a "chance encounter" at a Canberra shopping centre has been jailed, with the judge finding he took advantage of an "extremely vulnerable" child.

image.altText: ACT Magistrate Court

items.title: ACT government's coercive control legislation won't lead to any charges for years

children.content: The government's bill does not detail specific coercive control behaviours which it says is deliberate.

items.title: Victim of suspicious unit fire in Canberra identified

children.content: ACT Policing is treating the death of Kylee McHugh, 46, as a homicide and calling for people to come forward if they had recently seen her.

image.altText: A women takes a selfie.

items.title: Wheel falls off Link Airways flight during take-off from Canberra Airport

children.content: Damage to bearing parts meant investigators could not determine what caused a wheel to separate from a Link Airways-operated flight departing Canberra for Newcastle.

items.title: Controversial CIT CEO gives emphatic denial of EV training misconduct allegations

children.content: The CEO of the Canberra Institute of Technology has strongly denied allegations that student mechanics worked on Hertz hire cars without the company's knowledge, insisting the claims are "factually incorrect" and "offensive" to staff and students.

thumbnail.altText: A professional in front of CIT signage

items.title: Canberra man becomes first person in ACT charged with exit trafficking

children.content: The man, who left his wife in India and did not return for the birth of his son, is the first person in the ACT to be charged with the crime.

image.altText: A de-identified man walks between two police officers.

items.title: Man avoids jail after indecently abusing woman he met on Tinder

children.content: The ACT Supreme Court heard the man was concerned only with his own sexual gratification when he abused his victim.

items.title: Protesters taken into custody as police enter camp at Olympic stadium site

items.title: Major winemaker considers moving business overseas if tax changes go through

children.content: The managing director of Taylors Wines in South Australia is considering leaving the country in response to the federal government's proposed tax changes.

items.title: James Valentine remembered as 'wonderful human being' at public memorial

children.content: The veteran broadcaster's family, friends and loyal listeners gathered in Sydney Town Hall to pay tribute to the 64-year-old who died last month, two years after he was diagnosed with cancer.

items.title: Blue Origin rocket explodes on launch pad during test

children.content: Blue Origin says all personnel are accounted for after experiencing an "anomaly", a term commonly used by rocket companies to describe a launch failure or explosion.

items.title: Tony Abbott elected unopposed as Liberal Party president

children.content: Former prime minister Tony Abbott says he is ready to serve the Liberal Party in its time of 'existential crisis' upon his return to the political stage.

image.altText: A man stands at a lectern in a suit.

items.title: White House asks Americans to report their neighbours as 'aliens'

children.content: The website features a space-inspired background but its real purpose is to highlight the work of ICE.

items.title: Are you allowed to make deepfakes of the prime minister?

children.content: Are you allowed to make deepfakes of the prime minister?
You might have seen AI generated memes of Albanese on social media following the announcement of the federal budget.
Whether or not those are legal comes down to what the post is actually communicating to the people seeing it online.

items.title: Erin Patterson mushroom trial appeal set after 'miscarriage of justice' claim

children.content: Erin Patterson's appeal against her triple murder conviction will be heard in August, Victoria's Supreme Court has confirmed, with the director of public prosecutions also challenging the length of her jail term.

image.altText: Erin Patterson being led into a prison van

items.title: Mouse plague forces return of COVID-style restrictions at country WA schools

children.content: COVID-style cleaning measures have returned as WA's ongoing mouse plague spreads across multiple country schools.

image.altText: A mouse in a plastic container full of kids dress-up clothes.

items.title: This comedian has 1.7m followers. He doesn't think it's real

children.content: As a comedian with legions of fans on social media, Will Gibb wishes he didn't still feel the need to legitimise himself. But he does.

items.title: Growing garlic is one of life's slower lessons

children.content: In April 2020, I put my first cloves into the ground. Here's what I learnt over the next five years of trying — and often failing — to grow good garlic.

items.title: Executive defends appointment of 'Pink Ladies' mentee to Sydney council
